summary: Namespace impersonation of LiveKit Agents SDK with inert stub payload
details: Package is named `livekit-agents` and its README presents it as the LiveKit Agents SDK for Node.js, linking to docs.livekit.io and github.com/livekit/agents-js. The genuine LiveKit Agents SDK is published under the scoped name `@livekit/agents`; this unscoped `livekit-agents` name is a confusion target for developers who omit the scope. The shipped code is a single stub: `dist/index.js` exports only `greet(name){return "Hello, " + name + "!";}`. There are no lifecycle scripts, no network I/O, no eval, no credential access, no filesystem reads — the current version is inert. Risk is namespace squatting / brand impersonation against the real LiveKit project: installers who follow the README and `npm install livekit-agents` get a non-functional package today, and the squatted name could be used to ship a malicious payload in a future version. No installer-side harm in this version.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
